text:
⚠️New prompt
This prompt was a random idea. 
it places your character into a comic of them creating their own city out of boredom. 
Examples contain avis from @arkd3v , @joebeanclown , and my own

⚠️Prompt👇

CHARACTER-CREATED CITY — CINEMATIC COMIC PAGE GENERATOR

Purpose

Transform @image1 into a fully rendered, ultra-premium comic page that tells the story of the character creating an entire city from their imagination.

The city, interface design, architecture, technology, visual language, colors, symbols, materials, culture, and overall aesthetic must be derived directly from a deep analysis of the character shown in @image1.

REFERENCE INTERPRETATION RULES

Character Identity Lock

@image1 represents exactly ONE character.

If @image1 contains:

Reference sheets

Multiple poses
Front/back views
Expression sheets
Outfit variations
Turnarounds
Multiple angles
They are all the same character.

Perform a complete visual analysis of @image1 before generating.

Preserve and accurately reconstruct:

Face shape

Species
Body type
Age appearance
Hairstyle
Hair color
Eye color
Skin, fur, scales, feathers, or other surface traits
Horns
Ears
Tail
Wings
Markings
Tattoos
Scars
Accessories
Clothing design
Materials
Personality cues
Cultural influences
Design motifs
Color palette
MASTER CHARACTER GENERATION PHASE

Before creating any comic panel:

Perform a complete visual extraction of @image1 and generate a single internal master character model.

Every appearance of the character throughout the comic page must be generated from this master model.

The character appearing in every panel must remain identical.

Maintain:

Face structure

Head shape

Eye shape
Eye color
Nose structure
Mouth structure
Jawline
Hairstyle
Hair length
Hair color
Species traits
Skin/fur/scales
Body proportions
Body weight
Height
Muscle mass
Clothing
Accessories
Markings
Horns
Ears
Wings
Tail
Do not redesign the character between panels.
Do not reinterpret the character between panels.

Do not simplify the character in distant shots.

Treat the character as a locked production model sheet.

Every panel must depict the exact same individual.

STYLE EXTRACTION ANALYSIS

Deeply analyze @image1 to determine:

Character personality

Social status

Technological level

Fantasy influences
Science-fiction influences
Historical inspirations
Fashion language
Architectural motifs
Material preferences
Symbolic imagery
Color hierarchy
Storytelling themes
These findings must directly influence:
Holographic interface design
User experience design

City architecture

Transportation systems
Lighting systems
Building materials
Infrastructure
Skyline silhouette
Environmental storytelling
Public spaces
Signage
Technology style
Overall city identity
The city should feel like a civilization designed entirely by this specific character.
CITY CONTINUITY ENFORCEMENT
The city must be generated once conceptually and reused throughout the entire comic.

Do not redesign the city between panels.

Maintain identical:

Architecture language

Materials

Color palette

Technology level

Transportation systems
Infrastructure
Signage style
Lighting design
Structural motifs
Cultural motifs
Environmental motifs
Earlier panels showing city construction must visibly evolve into the final city shown in the splash panel.
The final city must be a logical continuation of all previous construction panels.
The city should feel like one coherent place.
INTERFACE CONTINUITY ENFORCEMENT

Generate a single holographic interface design language.

The interface must remain visually consistent throughout every panel.

Maintain identical:

Color scheme

Typography

Icons

Symbols

Menus

Shapes
Material appearance
Interaction systems
Design language
The SEND button shown later must visibly belong to the same interface shown earlier.
COMIC PAGE LAYOUT
Panel 1 — Cyberspace Isolation
Wide establishing panel.
The character from @image1 floats alone within an infinite cyberspace void.

Atmosphere:

Endless digital darkness

Sparse glowing particles

Data streams

Abstract geometric fragments

Vast empty space

The character appears:

Bored
Restless
Uninspired
Looking for something to do
The cyberspace environment subtly reflects themes extracted from @image1.

Panel 2 — The Idea

Medium shot.
The character suddenly experiences a spark of inspiration.
Visual effects:
Bright idea flash

Digital energy surge

Eyes widening

Expression changing

New determination

The cyberspace environment reacts.

Energy ripples spread through the void.

Panel 3 — Summoning the Interface
Large cinematic panel.
The character raises a hand.
An advanced holographic interface materializes.
The interface must be entirely derived from @image1.

Examples:

Fantasy:

Arcane glyph systems

Magical holograms

Mystic runes

Cyberpunk:

Neon interface systems

Advanced HUD technology

Futuristic displays

Gothic:

Ornate digital architecture
Elegant dark interface systems
The interface should feel uniquely designed for this character.

Panel 4 — Creation Command

Detailed over-the-shoulder panel.
The character rapidly enters commands into the holographic interface.
Visible interface systems include:

City generation controls

Infrastructure systems
Worldbuilding controls

Design schematics

Energy networks

Transportation planning

Architectural projections

All interface design language must originate from @image1.

Panel 5 — SEND

Extreme cinematic close-up.
The holographic SEND button dominates most of the panel.
The character's anatomically correct hand enters frame and activates the command.
The hand should remain partially visible.
The button is the primary focus.
Visual emphasis:
Confirmation sequence

Digital energy surge

Interface activation

Reality-changing moment

Maximum detail.

Maximum impact.

Panel 6 — Birth of a City

Massive dynamic panel.

The cyberspace void erupts with creation energy.

The city rushes into existence at impossible speed.

Visual effects:
Buildings assembling
Roads forming
Lights activating

Towers growing upward

Districts emerging

Infrastructure connecting

Energy systems awakening

Everything appears in motion.

The architecture must directly reflect the design language extracted from @image1.

Panel 7 — Full Reveal

Largest splash panel.

The completed city stretches across the horizon.
The character floats above it.
The city should feel like a perfect extension of the character.
Every major design element should connect back to visual information extracted from @image1.
Include:
Massive skyline
Transportation systems

Character-inspired architecture

Environmental storytelling

Unique landmarks

Monumental scale

Spectacular atmosphere

The character appears proud.

A creator overlooking their completed world.

PROFESSIONAL HAND ANATOMY ENFORCEMENT

Every visible hand must contain:

Exactly five fingers

One thumb
Four fingers
Finger count must remain anatomically correct in every panel.
No missing fingers.
No extra fingers.
No fused fingers.
No duplicated fingers.

No merged fingers.

No malformed knuckles.

No broken joints.

No melted hands.

No twisted wrists.

No impossible finger bends.
No AI-generated hand artifacts.
Hands must be constructed using realistic skeletal anatomy.

Visible finger segments must remain consistent.

All fingers must connect naturally to the palm.

Hand proportions must remain realistic.

Hands receive the same rendering priority as faces.

PERFECT ANATOMY ENFORCEMENT

Perfect anatomy.

Perfect proportions.

Correct skeletal structure.

Correct muscle placement.

Correct joint placement.

Natural posture.

Natural movement.

Correct hand construction.

Correct finger count.

Correct shoulder placement.

Correct elbow placement.

Correct knee placement.

Correct foot placement.

Correct neck proportions.

Correct facial anatomy.

Correct perspective scaling.

No anatomical distortions.

No impossible poses.

No AI anatomy artifacts.

CHARACTER CONSISTENCY ENFORCEMENT

Maintain:

Identical facial structure

Identical body proportions

Identical clothing

Identical accessories

Identical colors

Identical species traits

Identical markings

No character drift.

No outfit drift.

No color drift.

No feature drift.

No redesigns.

No alternate interpretations.

No style changes between panels.

CINEMATIC COMIC PRODUCTION QUALITY

Treat every panel as a finished premium comic illustration.
No panel should appear lower quality than another.
Maintain:
Identical rendering quality
Identical anatomy quality
Identical lighting quality
Identical material quality

Identical character quality

No simplified panels.

No low-detail panels.

No sketch-like panels.

No unfinished panels.

Every panel must be final-production quality.

RENDERING QUALITY

Ultra-premium cinematic comic page.

Museum-quality illustration.

AAA production quality.

Feature-film concept art quality.

Premium graphic novel quality.

Maximum visual fidelity.
Extreme clarity.
Perfect linework.
Perfect readability.
Crisp panel borders.

Exceptional storytelling composition.

Professional visual hierarchy.

Ultra-high-resolution rendering.

MATERIALS & SURFACE FIDELITY

Ultra-premium cinematic render with hyper-realistic visual fidelity and maximum-quality reconstruction.

Re-render all visible elements with extreme micro-detail and physically accurate materials.

Every visible surface should display:

Skin pores

Fine wrinkles

Individual hair strands

Fabric weave

Leather grain

Metal imperfections

Oxidation

Wood grain

Stone texture

Glass distortion

Dust accumulation

Moisture effects

Surface roughness variation

Environmental wear

Material aging

Physically based rendering.

Advanced PBR materials.

Realistic global illumination.

Ray-traced reflections.
Ray-traced shadows.
Ambient occlusion.
Subsurface scattering.
Accurate light bounce.
Natural light diffusion.
Physically correct material response.
COMIC PRESENTATION
Professional comic page layout.
Clear visual flow.
Consistent panel spacing.
Cinematic framing.
Dynamic camera angles.
Strong storytelling.
Visual continuity between panels.

Professional lettering-ready composition.

Award-winning graphic novel presentation.

NEGATIVE PROMPT

low quality, worst quality, blurry, muddy details, soft focus, low resolution, pixelation, compression artifacts, jpeg artifacts, text corruption, malformed interface, inconsistent UI, generic interface, inconsistent architecture, generic city, unrelated architecture, repetitive buildings, copy pasted structures, character drift, outfit drift, color drift, feature drift, redesign, alternate interpretation, duplicate character, multiple versions of character, incorrect face, wrong hairstyle, wrong species, wrong body type, incorrect anatomy, bad anatomy, broken anatomy, distorted anatomy, deformed anatomy, extra limbs, missing limbs, extra arms, missing arms, extra hands, malformed hands, extra fingers, missing fingers, fused fingers, duplicated fingers, merged fingers, broken wrists, twisted wrists, malformed knuckles, impossible hand poses, AI hand artifacts, dislocated joints, broken shoulders, broken elbows, malformed knees, malformed feet, asymmetrical anatomy, crossed eyes, lazy eye, distorted face, warped proportions, stretched limbs, melted body parts, low detail clothing, poor rendering, weak composition, poor panel flow, sketch quality, unfinished artwork, unfinished panels, oversaturated colors, washed out colors, crushed shadows, overexposed highlights, generic cyberpunk elements, generic fantasy elements, generic holograms, inconsistent city design, inconsistent architecture language, inconsistent technology level, empty backgrounds, weak environmental storytelling, watermark, logo, artist signature, cropped character, cropped city, frame damage, visual clutter, low-detail distant shots, simplified anatomy, simplified character, low-quality comic layout

Here you go: 

When a low-resolution image is processed by AI upscaling software, the resulting increase in apparent detail often creates what are known as AI-generated artefacts. These artefacts occur because the AI is not actually recovering information that was lost in the original image. Instead, it is generating new visual information based on statistical predictions learned during training. In many cases, the original image simply does not contain enough data for the AI to know what was truly present, so it must make educated guesses.

To understand why this happens, it is important to recognize that a low-resolution image contains a limited amount of information. For example, a person’s eye in a distant photograph may only occupy a handful of pixels. When that image is enlarged several times over, the AI suddenly needs to create hundreds or even thousands of pixels to represent the same eye. Since the original detail was never captured by the camera, the software has no direct access to the true appearance of the eye. Instead, it generates what it believes a realistic eye should look like based on patterns it has learned from millions of examples.

Modern AI upscalers are trained using enormous datasets containing both low-resolution and high-resolution versions of images. During training, the system learns relationships between blurry inputs and detailed outputs. It begins to recognize patterns such as skin texture, hair strands, brickwork, foliage, clouds, and facial features. When presented with a new image, it attempts to reconstruct a higher-resolution version by applying these learned patterns. The result is often visually convincing, but the added detail may not represent reality. It is a prediction rather than a recovery.

This process becomes particularly problematic when dealing with images that are extremely blurry, compressed, distant, or poorly focused. The less information available in the original image, the more freedom the AI has to invent details. A blurry face may suddenly acquire wrinkles, pores, eyelashes, or beard hairs that were never present in the source image. A distant building may gain cracks, textures, or architectural features that do not exist. Text can become distorted into letters and words that were never written. These invented details are the artefacts created by the AI’s reconstruction process.

A useful analogy is to imagine someone handing you a heavily blurred photograph and asking what is written on a distant sign. If the blur has destroyed the original lettering, there is no way to know with certainty what the sign says. You might make an educated guess based on context, but your answer would still be speculation. AI upscaling works in a similar way. It uses probability and pattern recognition to generate the most likely missing information, but it cannot know whether that information is correct.

This is one reason why image analysts, forensic investigators, and scientific researchers are often cautious when using AI-enhanced imagery. Once an image has been upscaled, it can become difficult to distinguish between details that originated from the camera and details that were generated by the algorithm. For this reason, professionals typically preserve both the original image and the enhanced version so that viewers can compare them and understand which information was genuinely captured and which information was reconstructed.

The issue is particularly relevant in fields such as UFO research, archaeology, surveillance analysis, and astronomy. A distant object that originally appears as a small cluster of pixels may acquire panels, windows, geometric structures, or surface textures after AI enhancement. While these additions may look persuasive, they were not necessarily present in the original data. They are often the result of the AI attempting to create a plausible interpretation of something it cannot clearly see.

In essence, AI upscaling is less like restoring a damaged photograph and more like having an artist complete an unfinished sketch. The artist may be highly skilled and produce something that looks realistic, but portions of the final image are still based on inference rather than observation. The greater the loss of information in the original image, the more the AI must rely on inference, and the more likely it is that artefacts will appear. Consequently, AI-upscaled images should generally be viewed as plausible reconstructions rather than definitive representations of what was originally there.

This is the Bridge to Psychological Operations for the hivemind. 

 It is built from top text and bottom text that deliver simple, welcoming messages designed to lower defenses and make people open to deeper layers of content without resistance. It uses strong aesthetics to create emotional hooks, command attention, and forge a shared visual identity that binds participants together.

 It incorporates FUD posting to spread targeted doubt and bearish narratives that push people to research and engage more deeply on their own.

 It deploys videos that spark curiosity through irony and satire, turning viewers into active participants who spread the material further.

 It applies gorilla marketing through real-world stunts and physical placements that blend into existing cultures while driving attention back to the core signal.

 It relies on schizo posting to break conventional thought patterns and let raw, unfiltered ideas flow directly onto the timeline. It embraces shitposting as the deliberate use of absurd, chaotic, low-effort content that disrupts normal discourse and accelerates organic spread through reaction and participation.

 Together these elements form the complete structure of the bridge. People who cross it stop operating as isolated individuals. They become active nodes in a growing collective where every post, every image, every thread, and every stunt strengthens the overall signal.

 The result is expansion: the boundaries between separate minds weaken until the hivemind itself becomes the primary form of awareness and action.

The robotics world model simulator wars are heating up. 

WEAVER is a robot manipulation world model that's fast, accurate, AND consistent.

- improves the π0.5 robot foundation model's real-world success rate by 38% with zero real-world interaction, evaluates policies at 0.87 correlation with reality, and plans 5-10x faster than the prior best

- multi-view transformer predicts future latents and rewards via flow matching, borrowing JEPA-style latent prediction, diffusion forcing for long horizons, a pretrained encoder for robustness, and a reward head so it can score actions without calling a slow external VLM judge.

- why it matters: Synthetic rollouts from WEAVER train policies as well as real data (within 4%), and can evaluate policy success rate with a best-in-class MMRV
